_comment: drupilot default configuration. Every DRUPILOT_* key can be overridden by an environment variable of the same name (env ALWAYS wins). See README, Configuration section.
_deterministic_comment: Reproducibility (DEFAULT ON). When true, drupilot freezes the versions/refs it resolves (Drupal core, the dev toolchain, the digests SHA, DDEV add-ons) into a per-project drupilot-lock.json (under the plugin's state dir, NOT the user's project tree) and reuses them on later runs, so porting the same module twice converges on the same toolchain and result. Set DRUPILOT_DETERMINISTIC=false (the escape hatch) to always resolve fresh (legacy floating behavior) and refresh the lock. This governs only drupilot's own resolution; the Claude Code permission mode still governs Bash/Edit/Write. Helpers: deterministic_mode / lock_get / lock_set / lock_resolve in scripts/lib/common.sh; capture/refresh via scripts/env/lock-sync.sh.
_autonomous_comment: Hands-off mode. When true (or invoked as `/drupilot <subject> auto`), the orchestrator runs setup -> assess -> port -> refactor -> test unattended: no initial confirmation, DRUPILOT_GENERATE_RULES is treated as 'auto' unless explicitly set to 'off', and the local .patch is always written at the end. It NEVER performs outward-facing actions on its own (no git push / Merge Request / contribute) — those stay opt-in via /drupilot-contribute. This only relaxes drupilot's own gates; the Claude Code permission mode still governs Bash/Edit/Write prompts (use acceptEdits or a headless bypass for a fully unattended run).
_core_target_comment: Drupal core compatibility decision (scripts/analysis/core-strategy.sh; helper recommend_core_target). DRUPILOT_CORE_TARGET_STRATEGY: auto (default) | d11-only | keep-d10. 'auto' keeps the widest set ('^10 || ^11') for a backwards-compatible Phase 1 port and switches to '^11' on a BC break / Phase 2 refactor. POLICY: a Drupal 11 port's PHP floor is the resolved DRUPILOT_PHP_TARGET (>= 8.3). Because Drupal 10 itself allows PHP 8.1, keeping D10 ALWAYS adds composer require.php '>=<target>' so a D10 + PHP<target site is blocked at install time (composer) instead of fataling at runtime; '^11' needs no require.php (core enforces its own minimum). DRUPILOT_KEEP_D10 is the legacy boolean override (true -> keep-d10, false -> d11-only), honored only when explicitly set in the environment.
_requirements_comment: Minimum versions used by preflight.sh. Some carry uncertainty (see PROMPT 1.2): the real gate is functional (e.g. that 'ddev config --project-type=drupal11' works), not just the number.
_php_support_comment: PHP support per Drupal 11 branch (PROMPT 1.2). 'unconfirmed' = versions not officially confirmed yet; 8.5 is NOT confirmed on any branch -> detect at runtime, never hardcode.
_digests_comment: Complementary AI-generated Rector rule layer (Dries Buytaert). Git repo, NOT a Composer package. NO LICENSE -> clone into a runtime cache, never vendor/redistribute.
_packages_comment: Composer constraints for the dev toolchain (PROMPT 1.1), installed inside DDEV. These are the verified SUPPORTED RANGES; the EXACT patch versions resolved on first install are frozen per-project in drupilot-lock.json (see _deterministic_comment) and reused while DRUPILOT_DETERMINISTIC is on. extension-installer only autoconfigures phpstan-drupal and upgrade_status only corroborates findings — neither affects the port result, so they carry a broad range and are pinned exactly by the lock rather than here.
_contrib_comment: URLs and endpoints for the Drupal.org contribution flow (PROMPT 3).
